req.title: Data retention (negative)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for Resource Retention Policy and Nightly Purge Job Requirements.

Requirements covered:
- Resources retained per configured policy
- Expired resources purged on nightly job
- Full audit trail maintained
- Negative paths: permission denial, missing mandatory elements, conflicting state transitions
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Models (production code interfaces assumed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ResourceState(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    PURGED = "purged"
    LOCKED = "locked"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending_review"


class AuditAction(Enum):
    CREATED = "created"
    RETAINED = "retained"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    PURGE_SCHEDULED = "purge_scheduled"
    PURGED = "purged"
    PURGE_DENIED = "purge_denied"
    POLICY_APPLIED = "policy_applied"


@dataclass
class RetentionPolicy:
    policy_id: str
    retention_days: int
    requires_approval: bool = False
    legal_hold: bool = False
    minimum_retention_days: int = 0


@dataclass
class Resource:
    resource_id: str
    name: str
    state: ResourceState
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime]
    policy_id: Optional[str] = None
    owner_id: Optional[str] = None
    tags: dict = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class AuditEntry:
    audit_id: str
    resource_id: str
    action: AuditAction
    actor_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    success: bool = True
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom Exceptions (production code interfaces assumed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionDeniedError(Exception):
    pass


class MissingMandatoryElementError(Exception):
    pass


class ConflictingStateTransitionError(Exception):
    pass


class PolicyNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class ResourceNotFoundError(Exception):
    pass


class LegalHoldViolationError(Exception):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def utcnow():
    """Fixed point in time for deterministic tests."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 2, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


@pytest.fixture
def standard_policy():
    return RetentionPolicy(
        policy_id="policy-standard-90",
        retention_days=90,
        requires_approval=False,
        legal_hold=False,
        minimum_retention_days=30,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def legal_hold_policy():
    return RetentionPolicy(
        policy_id="policy-legal-hold",
        retention_days=2555,  # 7 years
        requires_approval=True,
        legal_hold=True,
        minimum_retention_days=2555,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def short_policy():
    return RetentionPolicy(
        policy_id="policy-short-7",
        retention_days=7,
        requires_approval=False,
        legal_hold=False,
        minimum_retention_days=1,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def active_resource(utcnow, standard_policy):
    return Resource(
        resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        name="test-resource-active",
        state=ResourceState.ACTIVE,
        created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=50),
        expires_at=utcnow + timedelta(days=40),
        policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
        owner_id="user-001",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def expired_resource(utcnow, standard_policy):
    return Resource(
        resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        name="test-resource-expired",
        state=ResourceState.EXPIRED,
        created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=100),
        expires_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=10),
        policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
        owner_id="user-001",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def locked_resource(utcnow, legal_hold_policy):
    return Resource(
        resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        name="test-resource-locked",
        state=ResourceState.LOCKED,
        created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=200),
        expires_at=utcnow + timedelta(days=2355),
        policy_id=legal_hold_policy.policy_id,
        owner_id="user-002",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def mock_resource_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_id = MagicMock()
    repo.find_expired = MagicMock(return_value=[])
    repo.save = MagicMock()
    repo.delete = MagicMock()
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.save = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_resource_id = MagicMock(return_value=[])
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_policy_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_id = MagicMock()
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_permission_service():
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.can_purge = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    svc.can_apply_policy = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    svc.can_view_audit = MagicMock(return_value=True)
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def retention_service(
    mock_resource_repository,
    mock_audit_repository,
    mock_policy_repository,
    mock_permission_service,
    mock_notification_service,
):
    """
    Constructs the system-under-test.  Import from your production module;
    here we build a thin stand-in so the test file is self-contained.
    """
    from retention_service import RetentionService  # noqa: PLC0415

    return RetentionService(
        resource_repo=mock_resource_repository,
        audit_repo=mock_audit_repository,
        policy_repo=mock_policy_repository,
        permission_service=mock_permission_service,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def purge_job(
    mock_resource_repository,
    mock_audit_repository,
    mock_policy_repository,
    mock_permission_service,
    mock_notification_service,
):
    from nightly_purge_job import NightlyPurgeJob  # noqa: PLC0415

    return NightlyPurgeJob(
        resource_repo=mock_resource_repository,
        audit_repo=mock_audit_repository,
        policy_repo=mock_policy_repository,
        permission_service=mock_permission_service,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper builders
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_audit_entry(resource_id, action=AuditAction.PURGED, actor="system"):
    return AuditEntry(
        audit_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        resource_id=resource_id,
        action=action,
        actor_id=actor,
        timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        success=True,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. RETENTION POLICY APPLICATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestRetentionPolicyApplication:

    def test_active_resource_not_purged_before_expiry(
        self, purge_job, mock_resource_repository, active_resource, utcnow
    ):
        """Resources within retention window must NOT be purged."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = []

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            result = purge_job.run()

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_not_called()
        assert result.purged_count == 0

    def test_resource_expiry_date_set_from_policy(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        standard_policy,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Expiry date must equal created_at + policy.retention_days."""
        resource = Resource(
            resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            name="new-resource",
            state=ResourceState.ACTIVE,
            created_at=utcnow,
            expires_at=None,
            owner_id="user-001",
        )
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = standard_policy
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = resource

        with patch("retention_service.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            retention_service.apply_policy(
                resource_id=resource.resource_id,
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

        saved: Resource = mock_resource_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        expected_expiry = utcnow + timedelta(days=standard_policy.retention_days)
        assert saved.expires_at == expected_expiry
        assert saved.policy_id == standard_policy.policy_id

    def test_policy_applied_audit_entry_created(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        standard_policy,
        active_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Applying a policy must create an audit entry."""
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = standard_policy
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = active_resource

        with patch("retention_service.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            retention_service.apply_policy(
                resource_id=active_resource.resource_id,
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

        mock_audit_repository.save.assert_called_once()
        audit: AuditEntry = mock_audit_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit.action == AuditAction.POLICY_APPLIED
        assert audit.resource_id == active_resource.resource_id
        assert audit.actor_id == "admin-001"

    def test_minimum_retention_period_enforced(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        standard_policy,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Resources cannot be purged before minimum_retention_days."""
        resource = Resource(
            resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            name="new-resource",
            state=ResourceState.ACTIVE,
            created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=10),  # only 10 days old
            expires_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=1),   # technically expired
            policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
            owner_id="user-001",
        )
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = resource
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = standard_policy

        with pytest.raises(ConflictingStateTransitionError, match="minimum retention"):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=resource.resource_id,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_legal_hold_policy_blocks_purge(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        legal_hold_policy,
        locked_resource,
    ):
        """Resources under legal hold must never be purged."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = locked_resource
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = legal_hold_policy

        with pytest.raises(LegalHoldViolationError):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=locked_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. NIGHTLY PURGE JOB — HAPPY PATH
# ===========================================================================

class TestNightlyPurgeJobHappyPath:

    def test_expired_resources_are_purged(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """All resources past expiry date must be purged during the nightly job."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            result = purge_job.run()

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_called_once_with(
            expired_resource.resource_id
        )
        assert result.purged_count == 1

    def test_multiple_expired_resources_all_purged(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        utcnow,
        standard_policy,
    ):
        """Nightly job purges every expired resource in one run."""
        resources = [
            Resource(
                resource_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                name=f"expired-{i}",
                state=ResourceState.EXPIRED,
                created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=100 + i),
                expires_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=i + 1),
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
            )
            for i in range(5)
        ]
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = resources

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            result = purge_job.run()

        assert mock_resource_repository.delete.call_count == 5
        assert result.purged_count == 5

    def test_nightly_job_updates_resource_state_to_purged(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Resource state must transition to PURGED before deletion."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        save_calls = mock_resource_repository.save.call_args_list
        assert len(save_calls) >= 1
        saved_resource: Resource = save_calls[0][0][0]
        assert saved_resource.state == ResourceState.PURGED

    def test_nightly_job_runs_at_scheduled_time(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Job must respect its schedule and not run outside window."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = []
        scheduled_time = utcnow.replace(hour=2, minute=0, second=0)

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = scheduled_time
            result = purge_job.run()

        assert result.started_at.hour == 2
        assert result.success is True

    def test_job_result_contains_summary_statistics(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Job result must include counts for purged, skipped, and errors."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            result = purge_job.run()

        assert hasattr(result, "purged_count")
        assert hasattr(result, "skipped_count")
        assert hasattr(result, "error_count")
        assert hasattr(result, "started_at")
        assert hasattr(result, "finished_at")


# ===========================================================================
# 3. AUDIT TRAIL
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditTrail:

    def test_purge_creates_audit_entry(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Each purged resource must have a corresponding audit entry."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        mock_audit_repository.save.assert_called()
        audit: AuditEntry = mock_audit_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit.action == AuditAction.PURGED
        assert audit.resource_id == expired_resource.resource_id

    def test_audit_entry_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Audit entries must record the actor (system or user) that triggered purge."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        audit: AuditEntry = mock_audit_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit.actor_id is not None
        assert audit.actor_id != ""

    def test_audit_entry_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Audit timestamp must be populated and timezone-aware."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        audit: AuditEntry = mock_audit_repository.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit.timestamp is not None
        assert audit.timestamp.tzinfo is not None

    def test_audit_trail_preserved_for_denied_purge(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Denied purge attempts must also produce an audit entry."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]
        mock_permission_service.can_purge.return_value = False

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        audit_calls = [
            call_args[0][0]
            for call_args in mock_audit_repository.save.call_args_list
        ]
        denied_entries = [e for e in audit_calls if e.action == AuditAction.PURGE_DENIED]
        assert len(denied_entries) >= 1
        assert denied_entries[0].success is False

    def test_audit_trail_ordered_chronologically(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        standard_policy,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Audit log retrieval must return entries in chronological order."""
        entries = [
            AuditEntry(
                audit_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                action=AuditAction.CREATED,
                actor_id="user-001",
                timestamp=utcnow - timedelta(days=100),
            ),
            AuditEntry(
                audit_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                action=AuditAction.EXPIRED,
                actor_id="system",
                timestamp=utcnow - timedelta(days=10),
            ),
            AuditEntry(
                audit_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                action=AuditAction.PURGED,
                actor_id="system",
                timestamp=utcnow,
            ),
        ]
        mock_audit_repository.find_by_resource_id.return_value = entries

        audit_log = retention_service.get_audit_trail(
            resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
            actor_id="admin-001",
        )

        timestamps = [e.timestamp for e in audit_log]
        assert timestamps == sorted(timestamps)

    def test_each_resource_has_independent_audit_trail(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        utcnow,
        standard_policy,
    ):
        """Audit entries must be scoped to individual resources."""
        r1_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        r2_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        resources = [
            Resource(
                resource_id=r1_id,
                name="res-1",
                state=ResourceState.EXPIRED,
                created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=100),
                expires_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=5),
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
            ),
            Resource(
                resource_id=r2_id,
                name="res-2",
                state=ResourceState.EXPIRED,
                created_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=95),
                expires_at=utcnow - timedelta(days=3),
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
            ),
        ]
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = resources

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            purge_job.run()

        saved_audits = [call_args[0][0] for call_args in mock_audit_repository.save.call_args_list]
        resource_ids_in_audit = {e.resource_id for e in saved_audits}
        assert r1_id in resource_ids_in_audit
        assert r2_id in resource_ids_in_audit


# ===========================================================================
# 4. NEGATIVE PATH — PERMISSION DENIAL
# ===========================================================================

class TestPermissionDenial:

    def test_unprivileged_user_cannot_purge_resource(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
    ):
        """Purge attempt by unauthorized actor must raise PermissionDeniedError."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = expired_resource
        mock_permission_service.can_purge.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id="unprivileged-user",
            )

    def test_permission_denial_does_not_delete_resource(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
    ):
        """On permission denial, resource must remain intact in repository."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = expired_resource
        mock_permission_service.can_purge.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id="unprivileged-user",
            )

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_nightly_job_skips_resources_when_permission_denied(
        self,
        purge_job,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
        utcnow,
    ):
        """Nightly job must skip (not crash) when system lacks purge permission."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_expired.return_value = [expired_resource]
        mock_permission_service.can_purge.return_value = False

        with patch("nightly_purge_job.datetime") as mock_dt:
            mock_dt.now.return_value = utcnow
            result = purge_job.run()

        mock_resource_repository.delete.assert_not_called()
        assert result.skipped_count >= 1
        assert result.error_count == 0  # skipped != error

    def test_unprivileged_user_cannot_apply_policy(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        standard_policy,
        active_resource,
    ):
        """Policy application must be restricted to authorized actors."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = active_resource
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = standard_policy
        mock_permission_service.can_apply_policy.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            retention_service.apply_policy(
                resource_id=active_resource.resource_id,
                policy_id=standard_policy.policy_id,
                actor_id="readonly-user",
            )

    def test_unprivileged_user_cannot_view_audit_trail(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
    ):
        """Audit trail retrieval must enforce read permissions."""
        mock_permission_service.can_view_audit.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            retention_service.get_audit_trail(
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id="readonly-user",
            )

    def test_permission_check_receives_correct_actor(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
        expired_resource,
    ):
        """Permission service must be called with the exact actor making the request."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = expired_resource
        actor = "specific-actor-999"

        try:
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id=actor,
            )
        except Exception:
            pass

        mock_permission_service.can_purge.assert_called_once_with(
            actor_id=actor,
            resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. NEGATIVE PATH — MISSING MANDATORY ELEMENTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestMissingMandatoryElements:

    def test_purge_requires_resource_id(self, retention_service):
        """Purge call without resource_id must raise MissingMandatoryElementError."""
        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="resource_id"):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=None,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

    def test_purge_requires_actor_id(self, retention_service, expired_resource):
        """Purge call without actor_id must raise MissingMandatoryElementError."""
        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="actor_id"):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id=expired_resource.resource_id,
                actor_id=None,
            )

    def test_apply_policy_requires_policy_id(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        active_resource,
    ):
        """Applying policy without policy_id must raise MissingMandatoryElementError."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = active_resource

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="policy_id"):
            retention_service.apply_policy(
                resource_id=active_resource.resource_id,
                policy_id=None,
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

    def test_policy_without_retention_days_is_rejected(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
        mock_policy_repository,
        active_resource,
    ):
        """A policy missing retention_days must be rejected at application time."""
        bad_policy = RetentionPolicy(
            policy_id="bad-policy",
            retention_days=0,  # invalid
        )
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = active_resource
        mock_policy_repository.find_by_id.return_value = bad_policy

        with pytest.raises(MissingMandatoryElementError, match="retention_days"):
            retention_service.apply_policy(
                resource_id=active_resource.resource_id,
                policy_id="bad-policy",
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

    def test_purge_nonexistent_resource_raises_not_found(
        self,
        retention_service,
        mock_resource_repository,
    ):
        """Purging a resource that does not exist must raise ResourceNotFoundError."""
        mock_resource_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(ResourceNotFoundError):
            retention_service.purge_resource(
                resource_id="nonexistent-id",
                actor_id="admin-001",
            )

"""
Pytest tests for Resource Retention Policy and Nightly Purge Job Requirements.

Requirements covered:
- Resources retained per configured policy
- Expired resources purged on nightly job
- Full audit trail maintained
- Negative paths: permission denial, missing mandatory elements, conflicting state transitions
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Models (production code interfaces assumed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ResourceState(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    PURGED = "purged"
    LOCKED = "locked"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending_review"


class AuditAction(Enum):
    CREATED = "created"
    RETAINED = "retained"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    PURGE_SCHEDULED = "purge_scheduled"
    PURGED = "purged"
    PURGE_DENIED = "purge_denied"
    POLICY_APPLIED = "policy_applied"


@dataclass
class RetentionPolicy:
    policy_id: str
    retention_days: int
    requires_approval: bool = False
    legal_hold: bool = False
    minimum_retention_days: int = 0


@dataclass
class Resource:
    resource_id: str
    name: str
    state: ResourceState
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime]
    policy_id: Optional[str] = None
    owner_id: Optional[str] = None
    tags: dict = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class AuditEntry:
    audit_id: str
    resource_id: str
    action: AuditAction
    actor_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    success: bool = True
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom Exceptions (production code interfaces assumed)

Requirements covered:

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class ResourceState(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    EXPIRED = "expired"

    LOCKED = "locked"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending_review"


class AuditAction(Enum):
    CREATED = "created"
    RETAINED = "retained"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    PURGE_SCHEDULED = "purge_scheduled"
    PURGED = "purged"
    PURGE_DENIED = "purge_denied"
    POLICY_APPLIED = "policy_applied"

@dataclass
class Resource:

    policy_id: Optional[str] = None
